name: GRW objective collapse
summary: Modifies the Schrodinger equation by adding stochastic spontaneous-collapse events that localize wave functions at random times with rate lambda ~ 10^-16 s^-1 and localization width sigma ~ 10^-7 m. For single particles, deviations from standard QM are negligible. For macroscopic objects (N ~ 10^23 particles), collapse rates are amplified by N, producing classical localized behaviour on perceptible timescales. Solves the measurement problem by making collapse an objective dynamical process, not a measurement postulate.

addresses_puzzles.note: Stochastic non-unitary dynamics produces unique outcomes without measurement postulate. Testable: predicts spontaneous emission of soft X-rays from charged particles (constrained by X-ray-quiet experiments, see Donadi et al. 2021 arXiv:2111.03831, which pressured original GRW parameters). Modified by CSL (continuous spontaneous localisation, Pearle 1989) and gravitationally-induced collapse (Penrose 1996, Diosi 1989).

introduces.note: Two new universal constants (lambda, sigma) - the most-constrained-by-experiment of all interpretation-modifying QM proposals.

history: Ghirardi, Rimini, Weber (1986), 'Unified dynamics for microscopic and macroscopic systems', Phys. Rev. D 34, 470. Generalised to CSL (Continuous Spontaneous Localisation): Pearle (1989); Bassi-Ghirardi (2003). Modern X-ray tests have pressed lambda below the original GRW value; Donadi-Bassi et al. (2021), arXiv:2111.03831 - 'Underground test of gravity-related wave function collapse' - excludes the simplest gravitational-collapse parameters.

references.citation: Ghirardi, Rimini, Weber, 'Unified dynamics for microscopic and macroscopic systems', Phys. Rev. D 34, 470 (1986)
